Worksheet.title: N_106_01_Phone_lock_Method_by_Progkey
Worksheet.description:
0.Prerequisites

For this test you need a PLEIADES device in SIP mode running on an OXE server.

1.Objective

The aim of this test is to check that user can lock the device by using creating and using a specific lock progkey, that correct information are displayed on lock screen

2.Configuration

System config field give information about the context of the test :
SIP : ALE300 / ALE400 / ALE500 / ALE-30 / ALE-20

steps.action: On Sip device create a "Lock" Progkey
steps.expected_result: The Progkey is created with : dedicated icon, default name "Lock"

steps.action:
This "Lock" ProKey can be: moved, copied, modified (renamed), deleted
Try each of these options.
steps.expected_result: The progkey works as expected.

steps.action: Press the "Lock" Progkey to enable the lock function.
steps.expected_result:
The device shows the locked screen.
LOCK icon in the center of the screen.
LOCK icon, next to the time in the topbar.
LOCK icon a the top left of the screen to Lock or unlock the device.
The screensaver starts between 1 minute and 1 hour, depending on the screen saver settings.
The screen saver bubble is visible and shows only the closed LOCK icon, the date and the time.
If other events notification are present they are displayed next to the LOCK icon. (number of missed and voice messages)
The bubble alternately appears with the closed LOCK icon and the with company’s custom icon.

steps.action: To unlock the phone, Press the dedicated Lock icon on the top left of the screen or use another method.
steps.expected_result: The unlock "code input screen" is directly displayed.

Worksheet.title: pense N_106_02_Phone_lock_Method_by_Screen_saver
Worksheet.description:
0.Prerequisites

For this test you need a PLEIADES device in SIP mode running on an OXE server, that screensaver give the information that device is locked and that correct information are displayed on lock screen (when exiting screen saver mode).

1.Objective

The aim of this test is to check that user can define the device lock by screensaver triggering to ""true"" and that screensaver triggering actually locks the device.

2.Configuration

System config field give information about the context of the test :
SIP : ALE300 / ALE400 / ALE500 / ALE-30 / ALE-20

steps.action:
Settings---user---device---lock
enable lock and auto lock

the auto lock delay is : x minutes or x hour
steps.expected_result: can save the configuration successfully

steps.action:
Wait x minutes or x hour 
Check that the device is locked.
steps.expected_result:
The device shows the locked screen.
LOCK icon in the center of the screen.
LOCK icon, next to the time in the topbar.
LOCK icon a the top left of the screen to Lock or unlock the device.
The screensaver starts between 1 minute and 1 hour, depending on the screen saver settings.
The screen saver bubble is visible and shows only the closed LOCK icon, the date and the time.
If other events notification are present they are displayed next to the LOCK icon. (number of missed and voice messages)
The bubble alternately appears with the closed LOCK icon and the with company’s custom icon.

steps.action:
Settings---user---device---lock

midify the auto lock timer
steps.expected_result: can save the configuration successfully

steps.action:
Wait x minutes or x hour 
Check that the device is locked.
steps.expected_result:
The device shows the locked screen.
LOCK icon in the center of the screen.
LOCK icon, next to the time in the topbar.
LOCK icon a the top left of the screen to Lock or unlock the device.
The screensaver starts between 1 minute and 1 hour, depending on the screen saver settings.
The screen saver bubble is visible and shows only the closed LOCK icon, the date and the time.
If other events notification are present they are displayed next to the LOCK icon. (number of missed and voice messages)
The bubble alternately appears with the closed LOCK icon and the with company’s custom icon.

steps.action: To unlock the phone, wake up it and press the dedicated Lock icon on the top left of the screen or use another method.
steps.expected_result:
The unlock "code input screen" is directly displayed.
![](index.php?/attachments/get/42750)

Worksheet.title: N_106_03_Phone_lock_Method_by_Minikeyboard
Worksheet.description:
0.Prerequisites

For this test you need a PLEIADES device in SIP mode running on an OXE server.

1.Objective

The aim of this test is to check that user can lock a device by using the minikeyboard (blue arrow + lock key), that correct information are displayed on lock screen, that once device is locked screensaver is activated after 10 seconds and that screensaver give the information that device is locked.

2.Configuration

System config field give information about the context of the test :
SIP : ALE300 / ALE400 / ALE500 / ALE-30 / ALE-20

steps.action:
Press the dedicated Lock key on Mini Keyboard.
![](index.php?/attachments/get/42147)
steps.expected_result:
The device is locked.
The device shows the locked screen.
LOCK icon in the center of the screen.
LOCK icon, next to the time in the topbar.
LOCK icon a the top left of the screen to Lock or unlock the device.
The screensaver starts between 1 minute and 1 hour, depending on the screen saver settings.
The screen saver bubble is visible and shows only the closed LOCK icon, the date and the time.
If other events notification are present they are displayed next to the LOCK icon. (number of missed and voice messages)
The bubble alternately appears with the closed LOCK icon and the with company’s custom icon.

steps.action: Press the dedicated Lock key on Mini Keyboard again or use another method.
steps.expected_result:
The unlock "code input screen" is directly displayed.
![](index.php?/attachments/get/42751)

Worksheet.title: N_106_04_Phone_lock_Method_by_Hold_key
Worksheet.description:
0.Prerequisites

For this test you need a PLEIADES device in SIP mode running on an OXE server.

1.Objective

The aim of this test is to check that user can lock a device by using the hold physical key when phone is idle, that correct information are displayed on lock screen, that once device is locked screensaver is activated after 10 seconds and that screensaver give the information that device is locked..
We will also check that using the hold physical key when device has an ongoing call holds the call and does not lock the phone.

2.Configuration

System config field give information about the context of the test :
SIP : ALE300 / ALE400 / ALE500 / ALE-30 / ALE-20

steps.action: In IDLE, press the Hold key.
steps.expected_result:
The device shows the locked screen.
LOCK icon in the center of the screen.
LOCK icon, next to the time in the topbar.
LOCK icon a the top left of the screen to Lock or unlock the device.
The screensaver starts between 1 minute and 1 hour, depending on the screen saver settings.
The screen saver bubble is visible and shows only the closed LOCK icon, the date and the time.
If other events notification are present they are displayed next to the LOCK icon. (number of missed and voice messages)
The bubble alternately appears with the closed LOCK icon and the with company’s custom icon.

steps.action: To unlock the phone, Press the Hold key or use another method.
steps.expected_result:
The unlock "code input screen" is directly displayed.
![](index.php?/attachments/get/42752)

Worksheet.title: N_106_05_Phone_lock_Method_by_Order_Code
Worksheet.description:
0.Prerequisites

For this test you need a PLEIADES device in SIP mode running on an OXE server.

1.Objective

The aim of this test is to check that user can lock a device by using the OXE lock order codet, hat correct information are displayed on lock screen, that once device is locked screensaver is activated after 10 seconds and that screensaver give the information that device is locked..

2.Configuration

System config field give information about the context of the test :
SIP : ALE300 / ALE400 / ALE500 / ALE-30 / ALE-20

steps.action:
To lock the device, enter the "order code".
This information is available on OXE.
The default is frequently "45".
steps.expected_result:
The device is locked.
The device shows the locked screen during 10 seconds, then starts the screen saver.
The screen saver bubble is visible and shows only the closed LOCK icon, the date and the time in the topbar.
If other events notification are present they are displayed next to the LOCK icon. (number of missed and voice messages)

steps.action:
To unlock the device, enter the "order code".
This information is available on OXE.
The default is frequently "45".
!!!!!This approach was dropped for security reasons!!!!!

Worksheet.title: N_106_06_Phone_lock_Lock_remains_after_reboot
Worksheet.description:
0.Prerequisites

For this test you need a PLEIADES device in SIP mode running on an OXE server.

1.Objective

The aim of this test is to check that a lock device that reboots is still locked when reboot is finished.

2.Configuration

System config field give information about the context of the test :
SIP : ALE300 / ALE400 / ALE500 / ALE-30 / ALE-20

steps.action: Use any method to lock the device.
steps.expected_result:
Device is locked.
The device is locked.
The device shows the locked screen.
LOCK icon in the center of the screen.
LOCK icon, next to the time in the topbar.
LOCK icon a the top left of the screen to Lock or unlock the device.
The screensaver starts between 1 minute and 1 hour, depending on the screen saver settings.
The screen saver bubble is visible and shows only the closed LOCK icon, the date and the time.
If other events notification are present they are displayed next to the LOCK icon. (number of missed and voice messages)

steps.action: On SIP phone, trigger a power loss. (Unplug and plug the phone's power supply)
steps.expected_result: SIP phone restarts

steps.action: Check that the lock state remains after reboot
steps.expected_result:
Device is locked.
The device is locked.
The device shows the locked screen.
LOCK icon in the center of the screen.
LOCK icon, next to the time in the topbar.
LOCK icon a the top left of the screen to Lock or unlock the device.
The screensaver starts between 1 minute and 1 hour, depending on the screen saver settings.
The screen saver bubble is visible and shows only the closed LOCK icon, the date and the time.
If other events notification are present they are displayed next to the LOCK icon. (number of missed and voice messages)

Worksheet.title: N_106_07_Phone_lock_Incoming_Call_to_Locked_Phone
Worksheet.description:
0.Prerequisites

For this test you need a PLEIADES device in SIP mode running on an OXE server.

1.Objective

The aim of this test is to check that a lock device behavior when receiving an incoming call (from a non critical number).

2.Configuration

System config field give information about the context of the test :
SIP : ALE300 / ALE400 / ALE500 / ALE-30 / ALE-20

steps.action:
Incoming call to the locked phone, the device ring.
Step:1
steps.expected_result: The phone rings

steps.action:
Incoming call to the locked phone, it's possible to take the call as long as the phone is locked.
Test all standard call pickup methods and check that all are possible.
Step:1
steps.expected_result: Possible to take the call.

steps.action:
Incoming call to the locked phone, show the phone Unlock Pin Request screen
Step:2
steps.expected_result: Unlock pin request screen on device.

steps.action:
Incoming call to the locked phone, do not display the caller's identity.
Step:2
steps.expected_result: No caller identity information on screen.

Worksheet.title: N_106_08_Phone_lock_Incoming_Call_to_Locked_Phone_from_Critical_Number_??????
Worksheet.description:
0.Prerequisites

For this test you need a PLEIADES device in SIP mode running on an OXE server.

1.Objective

The aim of this test is to check that a lock device behavior when receiving an incoming call from a critical number.

2.Configuration

System config field give information about the context of the test :
SIP : ALE300 / ALE400 / ALE500 / ALE-30 / ALE-20

steps.action: Lock the phone by progarm key / hold key / minikeyboard
steps.expected_result: Phone is locked

steps.action: Phone receives the incoming call from the normal number or emergency /guard
steps.expected_result:
1.NOT present the caller identity (no name or number)
2.show the unlock PIN request

steps.action: check the incoming screen:
steps.expected_result:
1.The user can take the call, stop the ringing or deflect to voicemail (if available).
2.No other deflect, no info.

steps.action: Answer the call and check the screen
steps.expected_result: The user can put on hold (only with the hardware button) or hook-on. (no information, no new call icon, no back)

steps.action: If input correct pincode during the incoming or the conversation:
steps.expected_result:
Phone display the full features of the standard :
the remote side's information: avatar / number

Worksheet.title: N_106_09_Phone_lock_Phone_Unlock_PIN_Request_Screen_Display
Worksheet.description:
0.Prerequisites

For this test you need a PLEIADES device in SIP mode running on an OXE server.

1.Objective

The aim of this test is to check that user can access to unlock PIN request screen on a lock device.

2.Configuration

System config field give information about the context of the test :
SIP : ALE300 / ALE400 / ALE500 / ALE-30 / ALE-20

steps.action:
Settings---user---device---lock
Enable the lock feature.

And lock the phone by program key / hold button / minikeyboard ...
steps.expected_result: Phone is locked

steps.action: To Unlock the device, first wake it up, then press the "Closed lock" icon top left.
steps.expected_result: The Phone Unlock Pin Request screen is displayed.

steps.action: To Unlock the device, first wake it up, then press the "Hold key" first left key.
steps.expected_result: The Phone Unlock Pin Request screen is displayed.

steps.action: To Unlock the device, first wake it up, then press the "Lock key" on minikbd
steps.expected_result: The Phone Unlock Pin Request screen is displayed.

Worksheet.title: N_106_10_Phone_lock_Switch_between_PIN_Secret_Mode_and_Visualization_Mode_on_Unlock_Screen
Worksheet.description:
0.Prerequisites

For this test you need a PLEIADES device in SIP mode running on an OXE server.

1.Objective

The aim of this test is to check that user can switch between secret and visualization mode on unlock PIN request screen in order to hide or see the entered PIN code digits.

2.Configuration

System config field give information about the context of the test :
SIP : ALE300 / ALE400 / ALE500 / ALE-30 / ALE-20

steps.action: Check that the password is always hidden at Unlock Pin request screen apparition.
steps.expected_result: The PIN code is hidden (only stars are displayed)

steps.action: When Unlock Pin request screen is displayed select the eye icon to show the PIN code you have entered or you will enter.
steps.expected_result: A new icon appears Eyes with a hat. And now the pin code is visible

steps.action: Now press the Eyes with hat icon to hidd again the PIN code
steps.expected_result: The PIN code is hidden (only stars are displayed)

Worksheet.title: N_106_11_Phone_lock_Secret_PIN_Mode_by_Default_on_Unlock_Screen_after_Reboot
Worksheet.description:
0.Prerequisites

For this test you need a PLEIADES device in SIP mode running on an OXE server.

1.Objective

The aim of this test is to check that secret PIN code mode is the default visualisation mode.

2.Configuration

System config field give information about the context of the test :
SIP : ALE300 / ALE400 / ALE500 / ALE-30 / ALE-20

steps.action: After device reboot. Check that the password is always hidden at Unlock Pin request screen apparition.
steps.expected_result: The PIN code is hidden (only stars are displayed)

Worksheet.title: N_106_12_Phone_lock_Secret_PIN_Mode_by_Default_on_Unlock_Screen_after_Screen_Saver
Worksheet.description:
0.Prerequisites

For this test you need a PLEIADES device in SIP mode running on an OXE server.

1.Objective

The aim of this test is to check that visualisation mode automatically switches back to secret PIN code mode (the default visualisation mode) after screensaver.

2.Configuration

System config field give information about the context of the test :
SIP : ALE300 / ALE400 / ALE500 / ALE-30 / ALE-20

steps.action: Lock device.
steps.expected_result: Device is locked and displays the lock screen.

steps.action: Press the lock icon.
steps.expected_result:
Device displays the phone unlock PIN request screen.
Device is in secret PIN mode.

steps.action: Switch to PIN visualisation mode and start to enter first PIN digits.
steps.expected_result: PIN digits are visible.

steps.action: Wait until screensaver triggers.
steps.expected_result: Device displays screensaver on screen.

steps.action: Exit screensaver and go to the phone unlock PIN request screen.
steps.expected_result: Device displays the phone unlock PIN request screen in secret PIN mode (you can enter several digits to confirm that they are hidden).

Worksheet.title: N_106_13_Phone_lock_Enter_Incomplete_PIN
Worksheet.description:
0.Prerequisites

For this test you need a PLEIADES device in SIP mode running on an OXE server.

1.Objective

The aim of this test is to check device behavior when user enters an incomplete PIN code (4 first digits for exemple).

2.Configuration

System config field give information about the context of the test :
SIP : ALE300 / ALE400 / ALE500 / ALE-30 / ALE-20

steps.action: Lock device.
steps.expected_result: Device is locked and displays the lock screen.

steps.action: Press the lock icon.
steps.expected_result:
Device displays the phone unlock PIN request screen.
Device is in secret PIN mode.

steps.action: Enter incomplet PIN code. ex: only 4 PIN digitsand wait.
steps.expected_result: Device keeps displaying the digits until screen timout is reached and goes back to lock display : device is locked and displays the lock screen.

Worksheet.title: N_106_14_Phone_lock_Dial_Unlock_Order_Code_Followed_by_Incomplete_PIN
Worksheet.description:
0.Prerequisites

For this test you need a PLEIADES device in SIP mode running on an OXE server.

1.Objective

The aim of this test is to check that locked device behavior after 5 wrong unlock attempts.

2.Configuration

System config field give information about the context of the test :
SIP : ALE300 / ALE400 / ALE500 / ALE-30 / ALE-20

steps.action: Lock device.
steps.expected_result: Device is locked and displays the lock screen.

steps.action: Enter Lock order code on dial pad followed by incomplet PIN code. ex: only 4 PIN digits.
steps.expected_result: Device keeps displaying the digits until screen timeout is reached and goes back to lock display : device is locked and displays the lock screen.

Worksheet.title: N_106_15_Phone_lock_Unlock_Try_With_Wrong_PIN
Worksheet.description:
0.Prerequisites

For this test you need a PLEIADES device in SIP mode running on an OXE server.

1.Objective

The aim of this test is to check that device remains locked when user enters a wrong PIN code.

2.Configuration

System config field give information about the context of the test :
SIP : ALE300 / ALE400 / ALE500 / ALE-30 / ALE-20

steps.action: Lock device.
steps.expected_result: Device is locked and displays the lock screen.

steps.action: Press the lock icon.
steps.expected_result:
Device displays the phone unlock PIN request screen.
Device is in secret PIN mode.

steps.action: Enter a bad 6 digits pin code
steps.expected_result: Device erase the bad digits. New pin code can be enter.

steps.action: No second PIN conde is entered.
steps.expected_result: Until the screen timeout is reached, the device goes back to the lock display: the device remains locked and shows the lock screen.

Worksheet.title: N_106_16_Phone_lock_5_Wrong_Unlock_Attempts
Worksheet.description:
0.Prerequisites

For this test you need a PLEIADES device in SIP mode running on an OXE server.

1.Objective

The aim of this test is to check that locked device behavior after 5 wrong unlock attempts.

2.Configuration

System config field give information about the context of the test :
SIP : ALE300 / ALE400 / ALE500 / ALE-30 / ALE-20

steps.action: Lock device.
steps.expected_result: Device is locked and displays the lock screen.

steps.action: Press the lock icon.
steps.expected_result:
Device displays the phone unlock PIN request screen.
Device is in secret PIN mode.

steps.action:
Enter a wrong PIN code.

Repeat 4 more time.
steps.expected_result:
Device is still locked.
Counter of bad attempts is incremented each time : 1/5, 2/5, 3/5, 4/5 and 5/5.

Device is now blocked, displaying the info "Wait 5 minutes before the next trial" on screen.

Worksheet.title: N_106_17_Phone_lock_Phone_Unlock_after_5_Wrong_Unlock_Attempts_Timer
Worksheet.description:
0.Prerequisites

For this test you need a PLEIADES device in SIP mode running on an OXE server.

1.Objective

The aim of this test is to check that user can unlock device after 5 wrong unlock attempts timer.

2.Configuration

System config field give information about the context of the test :
SIP : ALE300 / ALE400 / ALE500 / ALE-30 / ALE-20

steps.action: Lock device.
steps.expected_result: Device is locked and displays the lock screen.

steps.action: Press the lock icon.
steps.expected_result:
Device displays the phone unlock PIN request screen.
Device is in secret PIN mode.

steps.action:
Enter a wrong PIN code.

Repeat 4 more time.
steps.expected_result:
Device is still locked.
Counter of bad attempts is incremented each time : 1/5, 2/5, 3/5, 4/5 and 5/5.

Device is now blocked, displaying the info "Wait 5 minutes before the next trial" on screen.

steps.action: Wait 3 min and wake device up.
steps.expected_result: Device is still blocked, displaying the info "Your phone is blocked" on screen.

steps.action: Press the lock icon.
steps.expected_result: Device is still blocked, displaying the info "Wait 2 minutes before the next trial" on screen.

steps.action: Wait for at least 2 min more (to make 5 min and reach the 5 wrong unlock attempts timer) and wake the device up.
steps.expected_result: Device is locked and displays the lock screen.

steps.action: Press the lock icon.
steps.expected_result:
Device displays the phone unlock PIN request screen.
Device is in secret PIN mode.

steps.action: Enter the correct PIN code.
steps.expected_result: Device is unlocked and idle.

Worksheet.title: N_106_18_Phone_lock_Authorized_Actions_on_Blocked_Device
Worksheet.description:
0.Prerequisites

For this test you need a PLEIADES device in SIP mode running on an OXE server.
""Emergency"" number is defined on DM profile which device belongs to.
""Guard"" number is defined on DM profile which device belongs to.
""Operator"" number is defined on the DM profile to which device belongs.


1.Objective

The aim of this test is to check that locked device behavior after 5 wrong unlock attempts.

2.Configuration

System config field give information about the context of the test :
SIP : ALE300 / ALE400 / ALE500 / ALE-30 / ALE-20

steps.action: Lock device.
steps.expected_result: Device is locked and displays the lock screen.

steps.action:
Press the lock icon.
Enter a wrong PIN code.
Repeat 4 more time.
steps.expected_result:
Device is still locked.
Counter of bad attempts is incremented each time : 1/5, 2/5, 3/5, 4/5 and 5/5.
Device is now blocked, displaying the info "Your phone is blocked" on screen.

steps.action:
Dial 911 on blocked device.
And then release the call.
steps.expected_result:
Outgoing call is automatically launched to 911 (no need to launch with OK, handset key, ...).
Tested device displays an outgoing call to 911.
User has no other option than to end the call.
After releasing the call device is still blocked, displaying the info "Your phone is blocked" on screen.

steps.action:
Dial 112 on blocked device.
And then release the call.
steps.expected_result:
Outgoing call is automatically launched to 112 (no need to launch with OK, handset key, ...).
Tested device displays an outgoing call to 112.
User has no other option than to end the call.
After releasing the call device is still blocked, displaying the info "Your phone is blocked" on screen.

steps.action:
Dial the Emergency number on blocked device.
And then release the call.
steps.expected_result:
Outgoing call is automatically launched to Emergency (no need to launch with OK, handset key, ...).
Tested device displays an outgoing call.
User has no other option than to end the call.
After releasing the call device is still blocked, displaying the info "Your phone is blocked" on screen.

steps.action:
Dial the Guard number on blocked device.
And then release the call.
steps.expected_result:
Outgoing call is automatically launched to Guard (no need to launch with OK, handset key, ...).
Tested device displays an outgoing call to Guard.
User has no other option than to end the call.
After releasing the call device is still blocked, displaying the info "Your phone is blocked" on screen.

steps.action:
Dial the Operator number on blocked device.
And then release the call.
steps.expected_result:
Outgoing call is automatically launched to Operator (no need to launch with OK, handset key, ...).
Tested device displays an outgoing call.
User has no other option than to end the call.
After releasing the call device is still blocked, displaying the info "Your phone is blocked" on screen.

steps.action:
Press the Guard progkey (ALE-400/ALE-500) or the physical key associated to (ALE-20/ALE-30/ALE-300).
And then release the call.
steps.expected_result:
Call is launched.
Device displays an outgoing call to Guard, the only capability for the device is to end the call.
After releasing the call device is still blocked, displaying the info "Your phone is blocked" on screen.

steps.action:
Press the Emergency progkey (ALE-400/ALE-500) or the physical key associated to (ALE-20/ALE-30/ALE-300).
And then release the call.
steps.expected_result:
Call is launched.
Device displays an outgoing call to Emergency, the only capability for the device is to end the call.
After releasing the call device is still blocked, displaying the info "Your phone is blocked" on screen.

Worksheet.title: N_106_19_Phone_lock_2_Wrong_Unlock_Attempts_and_Wait
Worksheet.description:
0.Prerequisites

For this test you need a PLEIADES device in SIP mode running on an OXE server.

1.Objective

The aim of this test is to check that locked device behavior after 2 wrong unlock attempts and waiting (to see that wrong attempts counter is not reseted after the 5 wrong attempt timer).

2.Configuration

System config field give information about the context of the test :
SIP : ALE300 / ALE400 / ALE500 / ALE-30 / ALE-20

steps.action: Lock device.
steps.expected_result: Device is locked and displays the lock screen.

steps.action: Press the lock icon.
steps.expected_result:
Device displays the phone unlock PIN request screen.
Device is in secret PIN mode.

steps.action: Enter a wrong PIN code.
steps.expected_result:
Device is still locked.
Counter of bad attempts is incremented : 1/5.

steps.action: Enter a wrong PIN code.
steps.expected_result:
Device is still locked.
Counter of bad attempts is incremented : 2/5.

steps.action:
Wait almost 7 min.
Press the lock icon.
Enter a wrong PIN code.
steps.expected_result:
Device is still locked.
Counter of bad attempts is incremented : 3/5.

Worksheet.title: N_106_20_Phone_lock_2_Wrong_Unlock_Attempts_and_Reboot
Worksheet.description:
0.Prerequisites

For this test you need a PLEIADES device in SIP mode running on an OXE server.

1.Objective

The aim of this test is to check that locked device behavior after 2 wrong unlock attempts and reboot (to see that wrong attempts counter is reseted after reboot).

2.Configuration

System config field give information about the context of the test :
SIP : ALE300 / ALE400 / ALE500 / ALE-30 / ALE-20

steps.action: Lock device.
steps.expected_result: Device is locked and displays the lock screen.

steps.action: Press the lock icon.
steps.expected_result:
Device displays the phone unlock PIN request screen.
Device is in secret PIN mode.

steps.action: Enter a wrong PIN code.
steps.expected_result:
Device is still locked.
Counter of bad attempts is incremented : 1/5.

steps.action: Enter a wrong PIN code.
steps.expected_result:
Device is still locked.
Counter of bad attempts is incremented : 2/5.

steps.action: Reboot device.
steps.expected_result: Device is locked and displays the lock screen.

steps.action:
Press the lock icon.
Enter a wrong PIN code.
steps.expected_result:
Device is still locked.
Counter of bad attempts is incremented : 3/5.

Worksheet.title: N_106_21_Phone_lock_Dial_Lock_Order_Code_Followed_By_Wrong_PIN
Worksheet.description:
0.Prerequisites

For this test you need a PLEIADES device in SIP mode running on an OXE server.

1.Objective

The aim of this test is to check device behavior after user dial the unlock order code followed by a wrong PIN code (wrong attempt increment).

2.Configuration

System config field give information about the context of the test :
SIP : ALE300 / ALE400 / ALE500 / ALE-30 / ALE-20

steps.action: Lock device.
steps.expected_result: Device is locked, displaying the locked device screen.

steps.action:
Dial the Lock/Unlock code order followed by an uncorrect user PIN code.
!!!!!!This approach was dropped for security reasons!!!!!

Worksheet.title: N_106_22_Phone_lock_PIN_Reset_Request_STEP1
Worksheet.description:
0.Prerequisites

For this test you need a PLEIADES device in SIP mode running on an OXE server.

1.Objective

The aim of this test is to check that admin can define a new PIN code on locked device.

2.Configuration

System config field give information about the context of the test :
SIP : ALE300 / ALE400 / ALE500 / ALE-30 / ALE-20

steps.action: Lock device.
steps.expected_result: Device is locked and displays the lock screen.

steps.action: Press the lock icon.
steps.expected_result:
Device displays the phone unlock PIN request screen.
Device is in secret PIN mode.

steps.action: Press the Admin icon.
steps.expected_result:
Device displays the admin Authentication request screen.
Device ask password.

steps.action: Enter the Admin password
steps.expected_result:
Device displays create a PIN code request screen.
Device is in secret PIN mode.

steps.action:
Enter a new PIN code
Select the eye icon to show the PIN code you have entered.
Erase some digits and enter other digits.
steps.expected_result:
PIN code is not visible (stars are displayed)
A new icon appears Eyes with a hat. And now the pin code is visible.
The digits can be erased and enter again.

steps.action:
Valide the PIN code and Confirm the new PIN code
Select the eye icon to show the PIN code you have entered.
Erase some digits and enter other digits.
Press OK
steps.expected_result: The new PIN code is now available

steps.action:
Select the Auto-lock option and set the delay,
or uncheck the Auto-lock option.
Confirm your choice.
Press OK
steps.expected_result: New PIN code is available and Auto Lock also/or not.

steps.action: Test the new PIN code to unlock the device
steps.expected_result: New PIN code works

Worksheet.title: N_106_23_Phone_lock_PIN_Reset_Request_STEP2?????
Worksheet.description:
0.Prerequisites

For this test you need a PLEIADES device in SIP mode running on an OXE server.

1.Objective

The aim of this test is to check that user can ask for a temporary PIN code on locked device (which be sent by mail and will be valid for a limited amount of time (tbd)) in order to set a new personnal PIN code.

2.Configuration

System config field give information about the context of the test :
SIP : ALE300 / ALE400 / ALE500 / ALE-30 / ALE-20

Worksheet.title: N_106_24_Phone_lock_Lock_Order_Code_Modification_OXE_Side_Idle_Phone
Worksheet.description:
0.Prerequisites

For this test you need a PLEIADES device in SIP mode running on an OXE server.
A lock progkey must be defined on device.

1.Objective

The aim of this test is to check that lock feature (by progkey or order code) is still working after lock order code is modified on OXE side.

2.Configuration

System config field give information about the context of the test :
SIP : ALE300 / ALE400 / ALE500 / ALE-30 / ALE-20

steps.action: Device is idle.
On OXE server modify the lock code order.
steps.expected_result: Code order is modified on OXE.

steps.action: Dial the new Lock/Unlock code order.
steps.expected_result:
Device is locked.
Device displays the locked device screen.

Worksheet.title: N_106_25_Phone_lock_Lock_Order_Code_Modification_OXE_Side
Worksheet.description:
0.Prerequisites

For this test you need a PLEIADES device in SIP mode running on an OXE server.
A lock progkey must be defined on device.

1.Objective

The aim of this test is to check that user can still lock device after lock order code is modified on OXE side.
2.Configuration

System config field give information about the context of the test :
SIP : ALE300 / ALE400 / ALE500 / ALE-30 / ALE-20

steps.action: On OXE server modify the lock code order.
steps.expected_result: Code order is modified on OXE.

steps.action: Lock device
steps.expected_result:
Device is locked.
Device displays the locked device screen.

steps.action: Press the lock icon (ALE-400/ALE-500) or the physical key associated to this icon (ALE-20/ALE-30/ALE-300).
steps.expected_result: PIN code is asked

steps.action: Enter the correct PIN code.
steps.expected_result: Device is unlocked and idle.

steps.action: Lock device.
steps.expected_result:
Device is locked.
Device displays the locked device screen.

steps.action: On OXE server modify the lock code order.
steps.expected_result: Code order is modified on OXE.

steps.action: Lock device
steps.expected_result:
Device is locked.
Device displays the locked device screen.

Worksheet.title: N_106_26_Phone_lock_Dial_Allowed_Number_911_on_Locked_Phone
Worksheet.description:
0.Prerequisites

For this test you need a PLEIADES device in SIP mode running on an OXE server.

1.Objective

The aim of this test is to check that user can dial the 911 number on locked device.

2.Configuration

System config field give information about the context of the test :
SIP : ALE300 / ALE400 / ALE500 / ALE-30 / ALE-20

steps.action: Lock device.
steps.expected_result:
Device is locked.
Device displays the locked device screen.

steps.action: Dial 911 on locked device.
steps.expected_result:
Outgoing call is automatically launched to 911 (no need to launch with OK, handset key, ...).
Tested device displays an outgoing call to 911.
User has no other option than to end the call.

Worksheet.title: N_106_27_Phone_lock_Dial_Allowed_Number_112_on_Locked_Phone
Worksheet.description:

1.Objective

The aim of this test is to check that user can dial the 112 number on locked device.

2.Configuration

steps.expected_result:
Device is locked.

steps.action: Dial 112 on locked device.
steps.expected_result:
Outgoing call is automatically launched to 112 (no need to launch with OK, handset key, ...).
Tested device displays an outgoing call to 112.
User has no other option than to end the call.

Worksheet.title: N_106_28_Phone_lock_Press_Guard_Progkey_on_Locked_Phone_Screen
Worksheet.description:
0.Prerequisites

For this test you need a PLEIADES device in SIP mode running on an OXE server.
Define a Guard number on DM profile which device belongs to.

1.Objective

The aim of this test is to check that user can press the ""Guard"" progkey (the one on the left) on locked device.

2.Configuration

System config field give information about the context of the test :
SIP : ALE300 / ALE400 / ALE500 / ALE-30 / ALE-20

steps.action: Lock the phone.
steps.expected_result:
Device is locked, displaying the locked device screen.
Emergency progkey is displayed on the right progkey location.

steps.action: Press the Guard progkey (ALE-400/ALE-500) or the physical key associated to (ALE-20/ALE-30/ALE-300).
steps.expected_result:
Call is launched.
Device displays an outgoing call to Guard, the only capability for the device is to end the call.

steps.action: Guard device takes the call.
steps.expected_result:
Call is ongoing.
Device displays an ongoing call to Guard, the only capability for the device is to end the call.

steps.action: End the call.
steps.expected_result: Device is locked, displaying the locked device screen.

Worksheet.title: N_106_29_Phone_lock_Press_Emergency_Progkey_on_Locked_Phone_Screen
Worksheet.description:
0.Prerequisites

For this test you need a PLEIADES device in SIP mode running on an OXE server.
Define an Emergency number on DM profile which device belongs to.

1.Objective

The aim of this test is to check that user can press the ""Emergency"" progkey (the one on the left) on locked device.

2.Configuration

System config field give information about the context of the test :
SIP : ALE300 / ALE400 / ALE500 / ALE-30 / ALE-20

steps.action: Lock the phone.
steps.expected_result:
Device is locked, displaying the locked device screen.
Emergency progkey is displayed on the right progkey location.

steps.action: Press the Emergency progkey (ALE-400/ALE-500) or the physical key associated to (ALE-20/ALE-30/ALE-300).
steps.expected_result:
Call is launched.
Device displays an outgoing call to Emergency, the only capability for the device is to end the call.

steps.action: Emergency  device takes the call.
steps.expected_result:
Call is ongoing.
Device displays an ongoing call to Emergency, the only capability for the device is to end the call.

steps.action: End the call.
steps.expected_result: Device is locked, displaying the locked device screen.

Worksheet.title: N_106_30_Phone_lock_Dial_Emergency_Number_on_Locked_Phone
Worksheet.description:
0.Prerequisites

For this test you need a PLEIADES device in SIP mode running on an OXE server.

1.Objective

The aim of this test is to check that user can dial the ""Emergency"" number (if defined on system) on locked device.

2.Configuration

System config field give information about the context of the test :
SIP : ALE300 / ALE400 / ALE500 / ALE-30 / ALE-20

steps.action: Define an Emergency number on DM profile which device belongs to.
steps.expected_result: Emergency number is defined.

steps.action: Lock device.
steps.expected_result:
Device is locked.
Device displays the locked device screen.

steps.action: Dial the Emergency number on locked device.
steps.expected_result:
Outgoing call is automatically launched to Emergency (no need to launch with OK, handset key, ...).
Tested device displays an outgoing call.
User has no other option than to end the call.

steps.action: Emergency takes the call.
steps.expected_result:
Call is established, tested device displays an outgoing call to Emergency.
User has no other option than to end the call.

steps.action: End the call.
steps.expected_result: Device displays the lock device screen.

steps.action: Suppress the previously created Emergency number on OXE configuration (DM profile).
steps.expected_result: Emergency number is not defined anymore.

steps.action: Try to dial the previously defined Emergency number.
steps.expected_result:
As soon as device detect a unauthorized number :
- device displays the information message "Unauthorized number" on top orange banner
- the lock icon shakes to emphase that action did not trigger the unlock.
After 1 second, the numbering field is cleared.

Worksheet.title: N_106_31_Phone_lock_Dial_Guard_Number_on_Locked_Phone
Worksheet.description:
0.Prerequisites

1.Objective

The aim of this test is to check that user can dial the ""Guard"" number (if defined on system) on locked device.

steps.action: Define a Guard number on DM profile which device belongs to.

steps.action: Lock device.
steps.expected_result:
Device is locked.
Device displays the locked device screen.

steps.action: Dial the Guard number on locked device.
steps.expected_result:
Outgoing call is automatically launched to Guard (no need to launch with OK, handset key, ...).

steps.expected_result:
As soon as device detect a unauthorized number :

After 1 second, the numbering field is cleared.

Worksheet.title: N_106_32_Phone_lock_Dial_Operator_Number_on_Locked_Phone
Worksheet.description:
0.Prerequisites

For this test you need a PLEIADES device in SIP mode running on an OXE server.

1.Objective

The aim of this test is to check that user can dial the ""Operator"" number (if defined on system) on locked device.

2.Configuration

System config field give information about the context of the test :
SIP : ALE300 / ALE400 / ALE500 / ALE-30 / ALE-20

steps.action: Define an Operator number on DM profile which device belongs to.
steps.expected_result: Operator number is defined.

steps.action: Lock device.
steps.expected_result:
Device is locked.
Device displays the locked device screen.

steps.action: Dial the Operator number on locked device.
steps.expected_result:
Outgoing call is automatically launched to Operator (no need to launch with OK, handset key, ...).
Tested device displays an outgoing call.
User has no other option than to end the call.

steps.expected_result:
Call is established, tested device displays an outgoing call to Operator.
User has no other option than to end the call.

Worksheet.title: N_106_33_Phone_lock_Dial_Unallowed_Number_on_Locked_Phone
Worksheet.description:
0.Prerequisites

For this test you need a PLEIADES device in SIP mode running on an OXE server.

1.Objective

The aim of this test is to check that user can not dial an unauthorized number on locked device (another user number and an external number).

2.Configuration

System config field give information about the context of the test :
SIP : ALE300 / ALE400 / ALE500 / ALE-30 / ALE-20

steps.action: Lock device.
steps.expected_result:
Device is locked.
Device displays the locked device screen.

steps.action: Start to dial another user phone number on locked phone.
steps.expected_result:
As soon as device detect a unauthorized number (not Operator, Emergency, Guard, 911, 112) : 
- device displays the information message "Unauthorized number" on top orange banner
- the lock icon shakes to emphase that action did not trigger the unlock.
After 1 second, the numbering field is cleared.

steps.action: Start to dial an external number on locked phone.
steps.expected_result:
As soon as device detect a unauthorized number (not Operator, Emergency, Guard, 911, 112) : 
- device displays the information message "Unauthorized number" on top orange banner
- the lock icon shakes to emphase that action did not trigger the unlock.
After 1 second, the numbering field is cleared.

steps.action: Dial the 2 first digits of an authorized number (for exemple : 11 or 91) and launch call (press OK, handset key, picking up the handset, ...).
steps.expected_result:
- device displays the information message "Unauthorized number" on top orange banner.
- the lock icon shakes to emphase that action did not trigger the unlock.
After 1 second, the numbering field is cleared.

Worksheet.title: N_106_34_Phone_lock_Unlock_Phone_By_Dialing_Order_Code_and_Correct_PIN
Worksheet.description:
0.Prerequisites

For this test you need a PLEIADES device in SIP mode running on an OXE server.

1.Objective

The aim of this test is to check that user can unlock a locked device by numbering the lock order code followed by the correct PIN code.

2.Configuration

System config field give information about the context of the test :
SIP : ALE300 / ALE400 / ALE500 / ALE-30 / ALE-20

steps.action: Lock device.
steps.expected_result: Device is locked, displaying the locked device screen.

steps.action:
Dial the Lock/Unlock code order followed by the correct user PIN code.
!!!!This approach was dropped for security reasons!!!!!!

Worksheet.title: N_106_35_Phone_lock_Unlock_Phone_by_Entering_PIN_on_Phone_Unlock_PIN_Request_Screen
Worksheet.description:
0.Prerequisites

For this test you need a PLEIADES device in SIP mode running on an OXE server.

1.Objective

The aim of this test is to check that user can unlock device by entering the correct PIN code on dedicated screen (named : ""Phone unlock PIN request screen"").

2.Configuration

System config field give information about the context of the test :
SIP : ALE300 / ALE400 / ALE500 / ALE-30 / ALE-20

steps.action: Lock device.
steps.expected_result: Device is locked, displaying the locked device screen.

steps.action: Press the physical key associated to the lock icon (ALE-20/ALE-30/ALE-300) or press lock icon (ALE-400/ALE-500).
steps.expected_result: Device displays the "phone unlock PIN request screen".

steps.action: Enter the correct user PIN code.
steps.expected_result: Once the 6 digits correct PIN is entered phone is unlocked and idle.

Worksheet.title: N_106_36_Phone_lock_Delete_and_Correct_PIN_Digits
Worksheet.description:
0.Prerequisites

For this test you need a PLEIADES device in SIP mode running on an OXE server.

1.Objective

The aim of this test is to check that user can delete PIN digits if he entered wrong to digits and then enter the correct PIN code to unlock device.

2.Configuration

System config field give information about the context of the test :
SIP : ALE300 / ALE400 / ALE500 / ALE-30 / ALE-20

steps.action: Lock device.
steps.expected_result: Device is locked, displaying the locked device screen.

steps.action: Press the lock icon (ALE-400/ALE-500) or the physical key associated to this icon (ALE-20/ALE-30/ALE-300).
steps.expected_result: Device displays the "phone unlock PIN request screen".

steps.action: Start to enter PIN code.
steps.expected_result: "*" are displayed in the boxes.

steps.action: Press the delete arrow (ALE-400/ALE-500) or the physical key associated to the icon (ALE-20/ALE-30/ALE-300) to deleted several ".".
steps.expected_result: "*" are deleted according to the number of time arrow is pressed.

steps.action: Switch to PIN visualisation mode and enter digits.
steps.expected_result: Numbers are displayed in the boxes.

steps.action: Press the delete arrow (ALE-400/ALE-500) or the physical key associated to the icon (ALE-20/ALE-30/ALE-300) to deleted several numbers.
steps.expected_result: Numbers are deleted according to the number of time arrow is pressed.

steps.action: Enter the correct PIN code.
steps.expected_result: Device is unlocked and idle.

Worksheet.title: N_106_37_Phone_lock_Device_still_Locked_After_Reset_to_Factory_and_Rebooting_on_Same_System
Worksheet.description:
0.Prerequisites

For this test you need a PLEIADES device in SIP mode running on an OXE server.

1.Objective

The aim of this test is to check that device is still locked after a reset to factory made on device (during boot) and staying plugged on same OXE server.

2.Configuration

System config field give information about the context of the test :
SIP : ALE300 / ALE400 / ALE500 / ALE-30 / ALE-20

steps.action: Lock device.
steps.expected_result: Device is locked, displaying lock screen.

steps.action: Access to device settings and browse to "Advanced", "Maintenance", "Reset to factory" and launch the reset to factory.
steps.expected_result: Device processes the reset to factory.

steps.action: Let device reboot on same system.
steps.expected_result: The lock feature is disabled default

Worksheet.title: N_106_38_Phone_lock_Device_not_Locked_Anymore_After_Reset_to_Factory_and_Rebooting_on_Other_System
Worksheet.description:
0.Prerequisites

For this test you need a PLEIADES device in SIP mode running on an OXE server.

1.Objective

The aim of this test is to check that device is not locked anymore after a reset to factory made on device (during boot) and plug it on different OXE (or other kind of system) server.

2.Configuration

System config field give information about the context of the test :
SIP : ALE300 / ALE400 / ALE500 / ALE-30 / ALE-20

steps.action: Lock device.
steps.expected_result: Device is locked, displaying lock screen.

steps.action: Unplug and plug device back.
steps.expected_result: Device reboots.

steps.action: During device boot, access to device settings and browse to "Advanced", "Maintenance", "Reset to factory" and launch the reset to factory.
steps.expected_result:
input correct admin passowrd
Device processes the reset to factory.

steps.action: Before device boots, unplug device from system and plug it on another network (or power it up out of any network).
steps.expected_result:
Device is not locked anymore.
Personnal user data have been erased from device.

Worksheet.title: N_106_39_Phone_lock_Lock_Information_Displayed_on_Add_Ons
Worksheet.description:
0.Prerequisites

For this test you need a PLEIADES device in SIP mode running on an OXE server.

1.Objective

The aim of this test is to check that the only information displayed on add-on(s) plugged on a locked device is that device is locked.

2.Configuration

System config field give information about the context of the test :
SIP : ALE300 / ALE400 / ALE500 / ALE-30 / ALE-20

steps.action: Define several prokeys on tested device ("Number", "Voicemail", "Immediate forward", ...).
steps.expected_result: Progkeys are displayed on device and on add-on(s).

steps.action: Lock tested device.
steps.expected_result:
Device displays the lock screen.
Add-on(s) only dislay(s) the locked device information : previously created progkeys are not displayed on add-on(s).

steps.action: Unplug add-on(s), plug them back and wait until they are "up".
steps.expected_result:
Device displays the lock screen.
Add-on(s) only dislay(s) the locked device information : previously created progkeys are not displayed on add-on(s).

Worksheet.title: N_106_40_Phone_lock_Add_Ons_are_Blocked_When_Device_is_Locked
Worksheet.description:
0.Prerequisites

For this test you need a PLEIADES device in SIP mode running on an OXE server and which has 1 or more add-ons plugged on it.

1.Objective

The aim of this test is to check that add-ons are blocked on a locked device.

2.Configuration

System config field give information about the context of the test :
SIP : ALE300 / ALE400 / ALE500 / ALE-30 / ALE-20

en screen saver ou non

steps.action: Define several prokeys on tested device ("Number", "Voicemail", "Immediate forward", ...).
steps.expected_result: Progkeys are displayed on device and on add-on(s).

steps.action: Lock tested device.
steps.expected_result:
Device is locked.
the screen of the phone and addon: is opaque

phone display the lock icon but addon don't display the lock icon

steps.action: Press the add-on (s) physical keys associated to previously created progkeys even if it's in screen saver.
steps.expected_result: No action is triggered by the press.

Worksheet.title: N_106_41_Phone_lock_Lock_Screen_Progkeys_Display
Worksheet.description:
0.Prerequisites

For this test you need a PLEIADES device in SIP mode running on an OXE server.

1.Objective

The aim of this test is to check that device displays the correct progkeys on lock screen.

2.Configuration

System config field give information about the context of the test :
SIP : ALE300 / ALE400 / ALE500 / ALE-30 / ALE-20

steps.action:
Define "Emergency" and "Guard" numbers on OXE system for the DM profile the device belongs to.

Lock device.
steps.expected_result:
Device is locked, displaying the locked device screen.
Guard progkey is displayed on the left progkey.
Emergency progkey is displayed on the right progkey.

steps.action: Delete "Guard" number on OXE system for the DM profile the device belongs to.
steps.expected_result:
Device is still locked, displaying the locked device screen.
Emergency progkey is displayed on the right progkey.
Left progkey is blank.

steps.action: Delete "Emergency" number on OXE system for the DM profile the device belongs to.
steps.expected_result:
Device is still locked, displaying the locked device screen.
Left and right progkeys are blank.

steps.action: Define "Guard" number on OXE system for the DM profile the device belongs to.
steps.expected_result:
Device is still locked, displaying the locked device screen.
Guard progkey is displayed on the left progkey.
Right progkey is blank.

steps.action: Define "Emergency" number on OXE system for the DM profile the device belongs to.
steps.expected_result:
Device is still locked, displaying the locked device screen.
Guard progkey is displayed on the left progkey.
Emergency progkey is displayed on the right progkey.

Worksheet.title: N_106_42_Phone_lock_Locked_Phone_Upgrade_by_FTP
Worksheet.description:
0.Prerequisites

For this test you need a PLEIADES device in SIP mode running on an OXE server.

1.Objective

The aim of this test is to check that a locked device can upgrade itself when a new version is available on tftp server.

2.Configuration

System config field give information about the context of the test :
SIP : ALE300 / ALE400 / ALE500 / ALE-30 / ALE-20

steps.action: Lock device.
steps.expected_result: Device is locked, displaying the locked device screen.

steps.action: Copy new version on TFTP server and wait until define upgrade time comes.
steps.expected_result: Device upgrade itself.

steps.action: Wait until device upgrades itself and reboots.
steps.expected_result:
Device is still locked.
Check that new version has correctly been installed on device (by unlocking and goign in settings or with ssh connection and "id" command).

Worksheet.title: N_106_43_Phone_lock_Locked_Phone_Upgrade_by_USB
Worksheet.description:
0.Prerequisites

For this test you need a PLEIADES device in SIP mode running on an OXE server.

1.Objective

The aim of this test is to check that a locked device can upgrade itself by USB during boot when a version is available on USB key.

2.Configuration

System config field give information about the context of the test :
SIP : ALE300 / ALE400 / ALE500 / ALE-30 / ALE-20

steps.action: Copy the new version on USB key, and upgrade.auto empty file in root directory.
steps.expected_result: USB is ready for the test.

steps.action: Plug USB key to device and reboot device.
steps.expected_result: Device upgrade itself with new software.

steps.action: Let device upgrade itself and reboot.
steps.expected_result:
Device is still locked after upgrade.
Check that new version has correctly been installed on device (by unlocking and goign in settings or with ssh connection and "id" command).

Worksheet.title: N_106_44_Phone_lock_Access_to_Admin_Settings_During_Boot
Worksheet.description:
0.Prerequisites

For this test you need a PLEIADES device in SIP mode running on an OXE server.

1.Objective

The aim of this test is to check that user can not access to device settings on previously locked device during its boot.

2.Configuration

System config field give information about the context of the test :
SIP : ALE300 / ALE400 / ALE500 / ALE-30 / ALE-20

steps.action: Lock device.
steps.expected_result: Device is locked and displays the lock screen.

steps.action: Unplug device power and plug it back.
steps.expected_result: Device reboots.

steps.action: During boot try to access to device ADMIN settings.
steps.expected_result: Admin Settings are available during boot.

steps.action:
Phone has the pincode and enable the lock feature
Settings---user---device---lock---edit the pincode
steps.expected_result: turn to the current pincode screen

steps.action: input incorrect current pincode then press the ok key
steps.expected_result:
Popup  "Attempty incorrect x/5"
And can input pincode again

steps.action: Input correct current  pincode then press the ok key
steps.expected_result: Turn to the create pincode screen

steps.action: If press the later key
steps.expected_result: will not modify the pincode

steps.action: If input new pincode then press the ok key
steps.expected_result: Turn to the confirm screen

steps.action: Input correct new pincode then press ok key
steps.expected_result: turn to the auto-lock configuration screen

steps.action:
Enable/ disable auto lock 
or modify the auto-lock timer
then press the ok key
steps.expected_result: Save the configuration

steps.action: Check the lock menu and new pincode
steps.expected_result:
auto-lock : save successfully

Must input new pincode can unlock the phone

Worksheet.title: N_106_47_Phone_lock_User_Can_Lock_Device_With_Order_Code
Worksheet.description:
0.Prerequisites

For this test you need a PLEIADES device in SIP mode running on an OXE server.

1.Objective

The aim of this test is to check that user can lock a device by using the OXE lock order code (in order to prevent unwanted device lock).

2.Configuration

System config field give information about the context of the test :
SIP : ALE300 / ALE400 / ALE500 / ALE-30 / ALE-20

steps.action:
Try to lock the device, by entering the "order code" on dial pad.
This information is available on OXE.
The default is frequently "45".
steps.expected_result: Device is locked.

Worksheet.title: N_106_48_Phone_lock_User_Cant_Unlock_Device_With_Order_Code
Worksheet.description:
0.Prerequisites

For this test you need a PLEIADES device in SIP mode running on an OXE server.

1.Objective

The aim of this test is to check that user can NOT unlock a locked device by numbering the lock order code followed by the correct PIN code.

2.Configuration

System config field give information about the context of the test :
SIP : ALE300 / ALE400 / ALE500 / ALE-30 / ALE-20

steps.action: Lock device.
steps.expected_result: Device is locked, displaying the locked device screen.

steps.action: Dial the Lock/Unlock code order followed by the correct user PIN code.
steps.expected_result: phone is still locked

Worksheet.title: N_106_49_Phone_lock_Receiving_Call_When_Entering_PIN_on_Phone_Unlock_PIN_Request_Screen
Worksheet.description:
0.Prerequisites

For this test you need a PLEIADES device in SIP mode running on an OXE server.

1.Objective

The aim of this test is to check that user can unlock device by entering the correct PIN code on dedicated screen (named : ""Phone unlock PIN request screen"").

2.Configuration

System config field give information about the context of the test :
SIP : ALE300 / ALE400 / ALE500 / ALE-30 / ALE-20

steps.action: Lock device.
steps.expected_result: Device is locked, displaying the locked device screen.

steps.action: Press the physical key associated to the lock icon (ALE-20/ALE-30/ALE-300) or press lock icon (ALE-400/ALE-500).
steps.expected_result: Device displays the "phone unlock PIN request screen".

steps.action: Start to enter the correct user PIN code.
steps.expected_result: "*" are displayed in the boxes.

steps.action: Make a call to device from critical number.
steps.expected_result: Device display incoming call.

steps.action: Cancel call.
steps.expected_result: Device is locked, displaying the locked device screen.

steps.action: Make the same steps from step 2 but when call is established instead of cancelled.
steps.expected_result:
After ending the call.
Device is locked, displaying the locked device screen.

Worksheet.title: N_106_50_Phone_lock_PIN_Code_Modification_in_Device_Settings
Worksheet.description:
0.Prerequisites

For this test you need a PLEIADES device in SIP mode running on an OXE server.

1.Objective

The aim of this test is to check that user can modify his PIN code in device settings.

2.Configuration

System config field give information about the context of the test :
SIP : ALE300 / ALE400 / ALE500 / ALE-30 / ALE-20

steps.action:
Browse device to User Settings and modify PIN code to a new value.
Go back to homepage.
Settings --> Device --> Lock --> Edit PIN code
steps.expected_result: Device is idle.

steps.action: Lock device.
steps.expected_result: Device is locked, displaying the locked device screen.

steps.action: Press the physical key associated to the lock icon (ALE-20/ALE-30/ALE-300) or press lock icon (ALE-400/ALE-500).
steps.expected_result: Device displays the "phone unlock PIN request screen".

steps.action: Enter the new PIN code.
steps.expected_result: Device is unlocked and idle.
